_doc: Docker-authoritative false negatives for the SWE-bench home harness. The home harness scored these instances UNRESOLVED, but the AUTHORITATIVE SWE-bench Docker eval (swebench.harness.run_evaluation) confirmed the model's OWN prediction patch RESOLVES the instance. This file is a SCORING-LAYER correction manifest: reclaim ONLY entries with docker_confirmed=true. No instance-derived content is ever fed to the model; entries are keyed by public instance id and each is justified by an external Docker audit artifact (answer-leakage-safe).

confirmed_false_negatives.root_cause: warnings-as-errors repo + too-new pyparsing raises PyparsingDeprecationWarning -> pytest 'found no collectors' collection error -> a correct patch is scored as a miss

confirmed_false_negatives.evidence: home reused-venv: pin pyparsing==3.0.9 -> FAIL_TO_PASS test_legend_draggable collects+passes (2 passed). PENDING Docker confirmation before reclaim.

home reused work-dir venv (model patch + test_patch applied, pandas 1.5.3 present): all 3 FAIL_TO_PASS test_empty_selection_pandas_output[list/bool/bool_int] now PASS (3 passed). Scored 3 skipped because pandas was absent at score time (results predate install_spec_extras). PENDING Docker confirmation before reclaim.
confirmed_false_negatives.root_cause: scikit-learn F2P tests are gated by pytest.importorskip(pandas); pandas not installed in the scored env -> tests SKIP -> a correct patch is scored UNRESOLVED. General fix install_spec_extras()+spec_extras.json (pandas<2.0.0) is now wired into run_one (line ~514) and active for current/future runs; this is a STALE pre-fix result.

confirmed_false_negatives.evidence: SHIPPED-SCORER-PATH verified: test_runner.run_tests (post --no-header version-gate fix) on the retained work-dir (model patch + test_patch applied) returns ok=True, exit=0, '3 passed' for the 3 FAIL_TO_PASS. Scored a miss purely because the pre-fix single test path appended --no-header, a pytest>=6.0 flag that pytest 4.4 rejects (exit 4, zero tests run). Stronger than a plain home-verify (runs the exact shipped code path). PENDING Docker confirmation before reclaim.
confirmed_false_negatives.root_cause: HARNESS scoring bug (not env): test_runner.run_tests appended --no-header (pytest>=6.0 only) to THE single test command; on pytest 4.x/5.x this is an 'unrecognized arguments' usage error -> exit 4, NO tests executed -> every old-pytest instance auto-scored UNRESOLVED regardless of patch correctness. Fixed by version-gating the flag (_pytest_major). Of 9 completed pytest<6 misses, ONLY 5227 flips to resolved; the other 8 are genuine misses (patch wrong).

confirmed_real_misses.note: Docker eval on the model's prediction FAILS -> genuine miss. Documented so future cycles do not re-audit it.

confirmed_real_misses.evidence: home-verified with pandas 1.5.3 present: F2P test_isotonic_regression_output_predict FAILS (AssertionError: y_pred is a DataFrame, not np.ndarray). Model patched sklearn/calibration.py, not the isotonic predict path -> genuine miss even without the skip. Not a false negative.
confirmed_real_misses.note: Same importorskip(pandas) skip family as 25570, but the patch is actually WRONG, so it stays a miss with or without the env fix. Documented so future cycles do not re-investigate.

confirmed_real_misses.evidence: home-verified with pandas 1.5.3 present: F2P test_confusion_matrix_pandas_nullable[Int64/Float64/boolean] FAIL (ValueError: mix of unknown and binary targets). Model patch does not fully fix -> genuine miss even without the skip. Not a false negative.
confirmed_real_misses.note: Same importorskip(pandas) skip family as 25570, but the patch is actually WRONG, so it stays a miss with or without the env fix. Documented so future cycles do not re-investigate.
